content.raw:
Completed Roland Fleet Agentic Memory System Architecture V1.1 on DGX Spark GB10.

Storage: Hot tier (1TB NVMe SSD) for active notes and vector DB. Cold tier (8TB USB HDD) for archives and backups.

Stack: LanceDB for vectors, nomic-embed-text-v2-moe for embeddings, nemotron-3-nano for memory ops, nemotron-3-super for reasoning.

Four operations: Note Construction (LLM semantic enrichment), Link Generation (SUPPORTS/CONTRADICTS/EXTENDS/CAUSES/RELATED), Memory Evolution (versioned with confidence decay), Retrieval (vector + link expansion).

Phases 1-6 complete. Version tracking, confidence scoring, max 5 evolution hops. systemd timers for daily/weekly maintenance.

Load test: 103-271ms retrieval, 2.7s construction, 100% evolution accuracy.

semantic.context: Roland Fleet Agentic Memory System V1.1 architecture completed with storage tiers, stack components,

content.raw:
Article published on X/Twitter: "Building an Agentic Memory System for AI Agents: The Roland Fleet Experience"

Cognee failure story included - weeks of CUDA/ARM issues before finding A-MEM. A-MEM inspired lean implementation.

Key message: For local-first, privacy-preserving memory for agent fleets, A-MEM approach with production safeguards (versioning, rollback, confidence tracking) gets you most of the way there.

Benchmarks: 85-93% token reduction vs context-stuffing, 103-271ms retrieval latency, 2.7s note construction.

semantic.context: Building an Agentic Memory System for AI Agents using A-MEM to enable local-first, privacy-preservin

CTI Pipeline Status: Dark web observatory running with 25 workers. IOC validation needs BTC wallet regex fix (26-35 chars, 1/3/bc1 prefix).

Alert system: Cron at 6AM/6PM daily. Rules: Critical CVEs (CVSS>=9), Summit 7 brand mentions, APT activity, DIB sector targeting.

Recent IOCs: Volt Typhoon (PRC, AA24-038A, network equipment, LOTL), Stryker attack (CISA alert), CVE-2024-3094 (liblzma, CVSS 10.0, supply chain).

Workspace: ~/cti-workspace/ with graph DB at data/cti/cti.db.

content.raw:
X.com Analytics Baseline Established for @Deuslogica

Period: Feb 22 - Mar 21, 2026 (256 posts analyzed)

KEY FINDING: Threat intel threads (CVE/CISA KEV) achieve 3.4% engagement rate — nearly DOUBLE the 1.7% rate on other content.

Top insight: Reply chains outperform broadcast content. Highest-impression posts are conversation-driven, not monologues.

Content priority ranking:
1. CVE/CISA KEV breakdowns (3.4% eng rate)
2. APT group profiles with DIB relevance
3. Reply engagement with Tier 1 accounts
4. MSSP operations insights
5. Educational threads

Engagement rate: 1.86% (target: 2.5%)
Reposts critically low: 7 total (need more quote tweet with add)

Analytics file: memory/x-analytics-baseline.md

semantic.context: X.com analytics show threat intel content with 3.4% engagement, doubling non-CVE rates, with reply c

content.raw:
APT28 (Russia/GRU) exploiting Zimbra Collaboration Suite vulnerability (CVE-2025-66376) in attacks targeting Ukrainian government entities. 
        
CVE-2025-66376: Stored XSS vulnerability in ZCS allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. Patched November 2025.

Zimbra is widely deployed across DIB contractors and government agencies. Defense contractors using Zimbra for email/collaboration should patch immediately if not already on patched version.

Source: databreaches.net | MITRE ATT&CK: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

semantic.context: APT28 (Russia/GRU) exploited CVE-2025-66376 in Zimbra to target Ukrainian government entities.

content.raw:
Beast ransomware group toolset exposed on open server hosted by German cloud provider. Full TTP set exposed publicly.

Key finding: Beast shares TTPs with other ransomware gangs, suggesting shared development infrastructure or affiliate model.

Implications: Ransomware-as-a-Service ecosystem has common tooling that defenders can tune detections against once, apply broadly.

Source: databreaches.net | Actor: Beast (ransomware)

semantic.context: Beast ransomware group's toolset was exposed on an open server hosted by a German cloud provider, re

content.raw:
University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) cyberattack: 9-day service suspension after breach of Epic EMR platform. clinics statewide closed.

Third-party medical records vendor breach exposed patient data at Deaconess Health System (Western Kentucky).

DIB overlap: Healthcare + defense contractors often share infrastructure and have regulatory overlap (HIPAA, eventually CMMC for defense healthcare).

Source: databreaches.net

semantic.context: UMMC cyberattack caused 9-day service suspension due to Epic EMR breach; third-party vendor incident

content.raw:
Insider threat case: Cameron Curry ("Loot"), data analyst contractor at D.C.-based international technology company, convicted of 6 counts of extortion. Stole trove of corporate data including sensitive information.

Pattern: Contractor with data access exploited for personal gain. 

DIB relevance: Defense contractors with cleared personnel face insider threat from contractors who may have financial motivation or be recruited by hostile actors.

Source: databreaches.net | DOJ case

semantic.context: Cameron Curry, a data analyst contractor, was convicted of extortion after stealing sensitive corpor

content.raw:
City of Hamilton (Canada) ransomware attack: 80% of network crippled, $18.5M ransom demanded and refused. Services impacted from business licensing to fire department records.

Ransomware economics: Cities refusing to pay. Threat actors shifting tactics to data exfiltration + double extortion.

Source: databreaches.net | February 2024 incident

semantic.context: Hamilton, Canada hit by ransomware; 80% network down, $18.5M ransom refused.
